mechanism_narrative: The extremely low level of the federal funds rate (0.14%) indicates accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% and S&P 500 still produces positive returns in >60% of instances, or if Rank IC <0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate from its elevated level signals labor market improvement and economic recovery, which boosts investor confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.
failure_condition: When UNRATE drops by >0.5pp in 3 months, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in >50% of cases or have mean negative; or the signal does not outperform a dummy classifier.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and nonfarm payrolls simultaneously increase month-over-month, it confirms a broad-based economic expansion that is highly favorable for corporate earnings and equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and PAYEMS show sequential monthly increases.
failure_condition: If >40% of occurrences coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns, or if the joint signal is not significantly better than individual signals in a regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE above 5%), increases in retail sales and housing starts are particularly strong predictors of a recovery that boosts equity prices. This signal is only active when the economy is still in a high-unemployment recovery phase.
failure_condition: If the joint increase signal in RSAFS and HOUST produces negative median forward returns in the high-unemployment regime, or if the regime condition does not improve signal-to-noise ratio over a full-sample model.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 200,000 is a strong signal of labor market strength that drives equity prices up over the following week, as it indicates robust economic activity.
failure_condition: If more than 40% of instances where PAYEMS rises >200k produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal fails to generate positive mean returns over any 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is rising and the federal funds rate remains low (below 0.25%), it reflects a healthy economy without tightening risks, supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields negative median 20-day returns in >50% of months, or if the interaction term in a regression is not significant at 5% level over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in retail sales and real GDP signals strong consumer spending and economic output, which is a fundamental driver of corporate profits and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS monthly increase and GDPC1 quarterly increase occur.
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 30% of quarters, or fails to show predictive power in a time-series regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, any increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the beginning of monetary tightening that contracts equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25%, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in >60% of episodes, or if the signal fails to produce negative mean returns out-of-sample.
